videos.title: Viking Legacy of The Lake District
videos.description:
The area of Northern England now known as Cumbria was once part of Cumberland and was inhabited by Welsh people until the Anglo-Saxon king Edmund took over the area and later gave it to King Malcolm of Scotland in exchange for his military support. The area still has a great many Celtic names like Carlisle, Penrith and Penruddock. 

Many English people moved into the area too, though they were outnumbered by Welsh at first, there is also an Anglo-Saxon influence on Cumbrian toponymy with examples such as Keswick, Workington and Barrow-in-Furness.

Finally the Vikings settled the area in the 10th century, Norwegians came over from Ireland, Iceland and the Northern islands and left their mark too. Many mountains, rivers and valleys have Norse names, as attested by the abundance of the elements fell, -ay and dale (Mickledore, Scafell, Rothay, Duddon, Langsleddale, Allerdale). Many town and villages also contain Norse elements (Whitehaven, Ravenglass, Silloth, Ulverston, Ambleside) and the second largest lake, Ullswater is thought to be named after the Nordic god Ullr (Ull) as this video reveals. 

An excerpt from Grímnismál  mentions Ull, the mysterious god of hunting and skiing:

"May he have Ull's help, and of all of the gods,
who first takes hold of the flame;
for worlds fall open to the sons of the Æsir,
when they carry up cauldrons."

The video also shows Aira Force, a waterfall named by Vikings, the word "eyrara", meaning "gravel-bank stream or river" and  word force, from fors, is used in many parts of northern England as a synonym for "waterfall".

videos.title: Manor House Hotel - St Bees
videos.description:
http://www.manorhousestbees.co.uk  Tel: 01946 820587 
Situated in the heart of St Bees The Manor House hotel provides a warm and authentic welcome. We serve a variety of traditional home-cooked food through the day and flat screen TV, tea and coffee making facilities are available in all of the traditionally furnished bedrooms.

videos.title: A spectators view from Barrow Little Man descent | Fell & Hill Relay Championship, Keswick 2023
videos.description:
This was an epic day out to watch the 2023 National Fell & Hill Relay Championships. This year they took place close to Keswick in Braithwaite in the Lake District. 
There were 250 teams that took part and I went along to support my running club, Baildon Runners. 
The 4 legs were all different distances. Leg one and leg four were solo runs and legs two and three were run in pairs. Leg three was a navigation leg and the maps were provided a few metres after the start line!
The terrain was rough and incredibly boggy after storm Babet had blown through in the previous days bringing huge amounts of rainfall. 
You can see the results from the race here - https://www.sportident.co.uk/results/KeswickAC/2023/BritishFellRelay/

If you enjoy this video, we would LOVE you to subscribe you can do that with one click here and we can promise more excellent videos will follow! 
https://www.youtube.com/channel/UCXr9zuqMPkwFEzF5Q0nfPaA?sub_confirmation=1

If you would like to look at our Pretty Gritty kit, including the buff I have on in the video,  you can find it here https://www.prettygrittyfitnesshub.com/shop

If you are an active female and would like to be part of our friendly community on Facebook, please follow this link https://www.facebook.com/groups/prettygrittyfitnesshub/

If you would like to join our exclusive membership platform for active women or take a look at our range of beautiful fitness apparel, you can find out more here https://www.prettygrittyfitnesshub.com/

Disclaimer:  When performing any of the exercises featured on our channel, you are doing so at your own risk. The Pretty Gritty Fitness Hub, Katie Fitzgerald-Gill and Emma Stoney will not be responsible or liable for any injury or harm you sustain as a result of our fitness programme, including exercise videos and content on our websites. Any wild swimming featured in the videos is carefully assessed before entering and we would suggest you do the same. Thank you for your understanding.
